lines.text: Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are getting ready to go on their honeymoon. Monica is entering from the bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Babe! Aren't you excited we're going on our honeymoon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah I am!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (singing) Bermuda, Bahama, come on pretty mama...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right. Get it out of your system while we're alone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering with Phoebe) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Have a great honeymoon!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'd better go pack.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no, I already packed. The only thing I couldn't find though was your Speedo.
Joey looks at him, Phoebe tries not to smile, and Chandler is shocked.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A Speedo? Uh, I don't have a Speedo. I'm gonna go pack my regular long bathing suit. (Goes into the bedroom.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Oh good you're still here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I want to tell you to have a good honeymoon! (Hugs Monica.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And I also wanted you guys to know that I am telling the father today. (They all look at her expectantly) What? What? What?
Pause.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We know its Ross!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How?! How do you know?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It was his sweater, but-Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I so wanted Ross to know first, but I'm so relieved you guys know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so great! And I'm gonna be your baby's aunt!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know! (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me too! (Joins the hug.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm gonna be an uncle! Come here! (He joins the hug.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (breaking the hug) You're all gonna be aunts and uncles.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but I'm the only one related by blood.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Great! So now that you guys all know you can help me. Give me some advice on how I'm gonna tell Ross!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, what were you gonna say?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well I was gonna tell him that I'm-I'm gonna have the baby and he can be as involved as he wants.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well that, that sounds good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah but how do I start? I mean, what's-what's the first thing that I say? (They all pause to think.) Okay great! Thanks. (She starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! Good luck!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, bye.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bye.
Rachel exits.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey, what was that all about?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well I guess there is no harm in telling you now, Rachel and Ross are gonna have a baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (in a high pitched voice) What?! I didn't even know that-Why didn't you tell me?! (Pause) Why am I talking like this?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I didn't think you could keep it a secret.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (in the high pitched voice) What?! (Normal voice) I am an excellent secret keeper. I have kept all of our secrets.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What secrets?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no Joey, I am not going to tell you because I am an excellent secret keeper.
The girls walk away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (whispering to Chandler) You'll tell me later?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You already know.
Opening Credits
Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Phoebe are entering.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's so romantic to send people off on their honeymoon.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know, Monica and Chandler are married. Ross and Rachel are having a baby. Maybe you and I should do something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All in good time my love. All in good time. Oh shoot! I left my guitar in their apartment. Well you can let me in later.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't have a key, they took mine to give to you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! They took mine to give to you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why would they take away our keys?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe they don't trust us.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No that's not it. They let me keep my key the last time they were out of town.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You mean the time you broke the ketchup bottle and cleaned it up with Monica's guest towels?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I washed those!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No you didn't.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah that didn't sound like me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, what am I gonna do? I really need my guitar!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I have stuff in there too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What stuff?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica's chicken parm! I'll take care of it. (He picks up the phone) Hey Mr. Treeger, it's Joey Tribbiani. Listen, I need to get into Monica and Chandler's apartment. It's an emergency. (Listens) Uhh, gas leak! Yeah oh, and bring garlic bread. (Hangs up.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Did Rachel find you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh she was looking for you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh well, I guess I'll catch up with her later.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, she really wanted to talk to you now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it seemed pretty important.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think I might know what this is about.
Phoebe and Joey trade looks

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Uh, uh we promised we weren't gonna tell anybody this but uh, about a month ago Rachel and I slept together.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (deadpan) And?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! I thought you would be a little more shocked.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh sorry. (Shocked) And?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, we-we said we'd just do it that one time but, but now I think she may wanna start things up again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I don't think that's what it is.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why? What-what else could it be?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh wow, I don't feel well.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm telling you. I'm telling you. That's what it is. No wonder she was looking at me all funny during the wedding. She didn't say anything to you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Joey) Maybe it's something you ate?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Please, just-just, just go and talk to Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I guess I should. (Starts to leave.) Man, y'know what I have to realize? Maybe I'm just not the type of guy women can have just one night with. Y'know, they-they always seem to want a little bit more. I should remember that. (He pauses and then exits.)
The Airport Ticket Counter, Monica and Chandler are standing in line behind another couple kissing who are next in line to be served.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: Next?
The kissing couple doesn't move.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They're kissing let's just go around them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh honey, leave them alone, they're in love.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm in love too! But in an orderly fashion.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: Next?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to the couple) Hi! Can you do that and walk? 'Cause she said, "Next."
The couple moves up to the counter.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Sorry. We didn't hear you; we're on our honeymoon.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: Oh, let me see what I can do. (Checks the computer) There are some first class seats available.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Chandler) Did you hear that?! They bumped them up to first class because they are on their honeymoon! Come on! Let's act like we're on our honeymoon.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We are on our honeymoon.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Grab my ass!

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: Next?
They go up to the counter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi, sorry. I almost didn't hear you, because y'know I'm just so in love with my new husband. We're on our honeymoon.

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: Congratulations. Okay, Mr. Bing you'll be in 25J and Mrs. Bing you'll be in 25K.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no, you see we're on our honeymoon. So umm, can you do your little thing and bump us up to first class?

lines.speaker: Ticket Agent
lines.text: I'm sorry, all our first class seats are taken. That couple got the last two.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You see, if we'd gone around them like I said, we-She would've given us those tickets. Damnit!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: 25J and K, any chance those aren't together?
Joey and Rachel's, Phoebe and Joey are playing Rock 'em, Sock 'em Robots.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I still can't believe they took away my key. You trust me with yours.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course I do! And I'm gonna give it back to you as soon as they're done with it at the key shining place.
They hear a knocking sound coming from the hallway and go to investigate.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell is that?
They go into the hallway and see Mr. Treeger watching one of New York's bravest breakdown Monica and Chandler's door with an ax.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa! Whoa! Whoa! Treeger, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Mr. Treeger
lines.text: You said there was a gas leak in here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well why don't you use your key?

lines.speaker: Mr. Treeger
lines.text: Because by the time I find it on this thing (Holds up a huge key ring with a thousand keys on it), the whole place might have exploded. If that happens at another building that I manage, people are gonna start asking questions. (To the fireman) Come on! Hurry up.
With a final swing the door gives way.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! We could have done that.

The Airport, Chandler and Monica are following the previous couple through a tiny hallway that proves this is a set on a sound stage and not an actual airport, and see them enter the first class lounge.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look at that! Look at that! They're going into the first class lounge! Do you know what they have in there?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me neither! We have to get in! (She runs through the door with Chandler in tow.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Just act like you belong.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Oranges!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Shh! (To the guy behind the counter) Nice to see you again. (They tries to walk past him.)

lines.speaker: Airline Employee
lines.text: Uh sir, may I see your tickets please?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, of course. (Shows him the tickets.)

lines.speaker: Airline Employee
lines.text: I'm sorry, would you move your thumb? I can't see the seat number.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that's all right, I have it memorized. It's 1A.

lines.speaker: Airline Employee
lines.text: (grabbing the ticket from him) Sir, this is not a first class ticket. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Apology accepted. Excuse us. (They try to enter again.)

lines.speaker: Airline Employee
lines.text: Sir! I'm afraid I'm gonna have to ask you to leave.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine. (Starts to walk away then she runs over and grabs an orange before she exits.) Go! Go! Go!
The Hallway Outside Ross's Apartment, Ross is walking towards his apartment and sees Rachel sitting in front of the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (seeing him) Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. (He helps her up.) Umm, I think there's something that we really need to talk about.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (quietly confident) I think we do. Why don't we go inside? (They go inside.) Look uh, I know why you're here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, and to save you from any embarrassment umm, I think maybe I should talk first.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (warily) Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (He sits her down in a chair.) Uh, Ross and Rachel. Rachel and Ross. That's been one heck of a see-saw hasn't it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (confused) What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean look, that-that one night we had was fun and...and certainly passionate, but don't you think it's better if we just stayed friends?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Seriously. What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Okay. Y'know what? If you want to, we can do it one more time. I mean I'd-I'd be okay with that. In fact, I have some time right now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y'know what? Can I, can I talk now?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh sure. (He sits on the apothecary table and touches her hand.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (touches his knee) I'm pregnant. (Ross stops.) Ross? (Ross is staring off into space.) Ross? (Ross is still frozen) Okay, whenever you're ready. (Sits back and opens her magazine.) And you're the father by the way-but you got that...
Commercial Break
Ross's Apartment, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (closes her magazine) Can I get you some water?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm good. I'm good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, there is no pressure on you. Okay? I mean you can as involved as you want. (Ross nods.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I need uh... I'm just-I don't know-I don't understand, umm, how this happened? We-we used a condom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. I know, but y'know condoms only work like 97% of the time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? What? What?!! Well they should put that on the box!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They do!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No they don't!!! (He runs to the bedroom to check and returns with his box of condoms.) Well they should put it in huge black letters!!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Ross come on let's just forget about the condoms.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh well I may as well have!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Listen, y'know what? I was really freaked out too when I found out...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Freaked out? Hey no, I'm not freaked out! I'm indignant! As a consumer!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know what? Let's, let's talk later.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! I want to talk now! Okay? I-In fact, (picks up the phone) I am going to talk to the president of the condom company!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y'know maybe I should come back... (Starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (grabs her) Shh! Shh! Shh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (stops) Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on phone) Yeah I'll press 1! (Presses one which allows Rachel to escape.)
Monica and Chandler's, Mr. Treeger has finished inspecting Monica and Chandler's apartment.

lines.speaker: Mr. Treeger
lines.text: I've looked everywhere. There's no gas leak.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (eating) Huh. So then I can heat this up? (Goes and does so.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Treeger
lines.text: Anyway uh, I'll get moving on that new door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh great! And listen, could you do us a favor and not tell Chandler and Monica about this? 'Cause y'know umm, they don't-they don't have any kids of their own and-and this door was like a child to them.

lines.speaker: Mr. Treeger
lines.text: Well I'm gonna have to put on a new lock, they'll find out anyway.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Mr. Treeger
lines.text: (measures the top of the doorframe) Whoa! This looks like an all day job, I'll have to cancel my yoga class. (Ross walks up.) Hey Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Mr. Treeger
lines.text: Could you tell Jasmine that I won't make it to yoga class today?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Mr. Treeger
lines.text: Namaste. (Bows.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Namaste. (Bows.)
Treeger leaves and Ross notices the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! What happened to the door?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So it's noticeable huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, is Rachel here? I really need to talk to her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Didn't you two already talk?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah but uh... Okay, okay look you guys know that Rachel and I slept together, but there's something else. (Pause) Rachel's pregnant.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (simultaneously) Oh my God!!! I can't believe that!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (simultaneously) Holy mother of God!!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: With my child.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is brand new information!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You already know don't you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A little bit.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Okay. I mean I'll be okay. It's just I don't think I handled it very well.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, what did you say to her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nothing. But the complaint department at the condom company got an earful. And then when I turned around she was gone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But hey, in my defense I-I just found out condoms are like only 97% effective.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (shocked) What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I gotta go find her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa! Hey! Whoa!! Hold up! Are you serious?! So like 3% of the time they don't even work?! Huh? They should put that on the box!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Evidently they do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! (Grabs his condoms from his pocket and looks.)
The Atlantis Resort, Chandler and Monica are arriving to check in, but are behind the couple from before again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe we're here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh you've got to be kidding me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? (Monica sees the first couple and gasps.)

lines.speaker: Front Desk Clerk
lines.text: As a wedding gift to you, the hotel would like to give you the honeymoon suite.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!! You have been screwing us all day!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're you just ten seconds later!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! You already got the first class tickets; you got the lounge! I mean we should get free stuff too! I mean you're not the only ones on your honeymoon!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Well you can have the suite if you want. We don't care about where we stay. We're here to celebrate our love together. We don't have to get free stuff. We just want to be together.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looks at Monica then at them) We need the stuff.
Monica and Chandler's, Joey is on the phone and Phoebe is watching him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Monica it's Joey. Listen uh, Phoebe and I smell gas comin' from your apartment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? Are you serious?! (To Chandler) Joey smells gas!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What else is new?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah and we'd go check it out, but you took away our keys.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well do something! Get in there!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How? I guess I could break down your door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! Do that!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And-and you won't blame us for any damage? (Gives Phoebe a thumbs up.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! (Pause) Are you doing it?! I don't hear anything! Come on!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, okay I'll-I'll-I'll break it down. (He hands the phone to Phoebe, gets up, picks up a chair, and starts banging it on the floor.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hey hi, he's doing it. He's breaking down the door. (The chair breaks in half.) Okay, we're in. (She hangs up the phone.)
A doctor's office, Rachel is on an examining table with her legs in the stirrups.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Okay Rachel, are you comfortable?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sighs) If I said I was, would you judge me?

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: The doctor will be here in a minute to do your sonogram.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (The nurse exits.) Oh man, I swear if they sold these at Pottery Barn...
There's a knock on the door and Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (shocked) Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh we-we need to talk.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-uh-uh, right now? Because I've kinda got an el fresco situation going on over here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Please, please I want to apologize for the way I acted earlier today.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Ross that's fine, but can you please stand near my head?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Oh yeah. (He moves next to her head.) I'm sorry. I mean I-I think I went a little crazy. I mean I was thinking about myself when I (Wanders towards Rachel's feet) really-I should have been thinking about you Rach...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Head Ross! Head Ross! Head Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right! Right! I just-I want you to know that I'm going to be there through this whole thing, okay? Okay? The doctor's appointments, the uh, the Lamaze classes, uh baby-proofing the apartment-Although we could probably worry about that 'til after we get married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What married?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well yeah, I think we should get married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What, because that's your answer to everything?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, because that's the right thing to do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, maybe if you're in love. But Ross, we are not in love, are we?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No but...but still you can't possibly do this alone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on Rach, you can't even eat alone in a restaurant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm just saying if you can't eat by yourself, how do you expect to have a baby by yourself?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can too eat by myself!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: When have you ever?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: When certain people leave the table and I am not finished!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well certain other people take two hours to eat a bowl of soup!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh please, you inhale your food!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I grew up with Monica! If you didn't eat fast you didn't eat!!

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: (entering) Am I interrupting?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no Dr. Long, please come in. This is Ross, he is the father.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But not the husband, because evidently she can do this alone.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Huh. Nice to meet you. I'll get started on this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Rachel, standing by her feet) I don't know why you can't admit that you need me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I do need you! I need you to stand near my head!

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Okay, everything looks good. Here it is on the screen. (We see Ross and Rachel looking at the screen.) Here is your uterus. And right here is your baby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow. There it is, I see it.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Congratulations. I'll give you two a minute.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.
Dr. Long exits and Rachel starts to cry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pretty amazing huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't see it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't see it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You-you just said that you did!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I lied! I didn't want her to think I was a terrible mother! I can't even see my own baby!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh sure, come here! (Sits her up) Sure you can! Uh, look come here look, (rolls the machine closer) it's-it's-it's, it's right there (Points).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Oh, it's beautiful. I see it now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I don't see it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on! Come on! Here, okay-okay, you see this? (Points) This tiny thing that looks like a peanut?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sweetie that's it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's it? Well I saw that! Ohh-ohh-oh, thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! I can't believe that's our baby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, that's our baby.
Closing Credits
Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Phoebe are there as Rachel and Ross return from the doctor's appointment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! So how was the doctor?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, everything went great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh hey, show them the picture of your uterus.
She does so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't see the baby. Where is it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, I know I couldn't see it either at first, but it's right umm... (Starts to cry) Ross, I lost it again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. (He takes the picture and hugs her.
End
